data.title: US imposes 15% tariffs on EU wine and spirits hurting industry worth €30 billion. French wine exports to US at 41%, major impact expected.
data.description: US consumers would face higher prices on European wine and spirits as President Trump's trade deal with the EU locks in a 15% tariff on most EU exports. Despite lobbying efforts, the EU's €30 billion wine and spirits industry did not receive an exemption. The US Chamber of Commerce warned that tariffs could cost the typical household about $4,000 per year, affecting small businesses and households. France, the world's largest exporter of wine and spirits, faces potential impacts with €12.1 billion worth of exports in 2024. Bordeaux winemakers are preparing for the hit, as the US imports 30% of the EU's products and consumes 20% of Bordeaux's annual output, valued at €400 million.

data.title: Huawei hosts "Future of Gaming Salon - Germany Edition" in Cologne to connect Chinese and European gaming ecosystems.
data.description: In Cologne, Germany on August 28, 2025, Huawei held the "Future of Gaming Salon – Germany Edition" to bridge the gaming communities of China and Europe. The event, led by Huawei Game Center, brought together key players from the Chinese gaming sector.

data.title: Veeva Systems Inc. beats Q2 earnings estimates with $1.99/share, $789.081M sales. FY2026 guidance raised to $7.78 EPS, $3.134-$3.140B sales. CEO highlights vision of connected software and business consulting for life sciences.
data.description: Veeva Systems Inc., listed on NYSE as VEEV, exceeded expectations with its second-quarter earnings report. Earnings per share stood at $1.99, surpassing the analyst consensus of $1.90, and quarterly sales were reported at $789.081 million, beating the estimated $768.417 million. The company also adjusted its guidance for FY2026, increasing the adjusted EPS from $7.63 to $7.78 and raising sales guidance from $3.090 billion-$3.100 billion to $3.134 billion-$3.140 billion. CEO Peter Gassner expressed excitement for the company's growth and highlighted the potential of Veeva AI and Veeva Data Cloud in driving transformational change in the life sciences sector.

data.title: Cytokinetics grants stock options to 46 new employees - 105,169 shares & 70,879 RSUs, totaling 176,048 shares.
data.description: On August 15, 2025, Cytokinetics, Incorporated (Nasdaq: CYTK) based in South San Francisco, California, awarded stock options to buy a total of 105,169 shares of common stock and 70,879 restricted stock units (RSUs) to 46 new employees who started working in July and August 2025. The RSUs will be converted into common stock upon vesting. This action was taken as a significant incentive for their employment.

data.title: Chainlink's LINK price jumps 1.67% to $24.92, aiming for $27 resistance. Bulls in control with mixed signals.
data.description: The price of LINK has surged to $24.92, driven by a powerful bullish trend that has surpassed important moving averages. The digital asset is now eyeing a resistance level of $27.87 amidst conflicting signals from technical indicators.

data.title: GA completes the world's largest and most powerful pulsed superconducting magnet for fusion energy at nearly 60ft tall.
data.description: In San Diego, the team at General Atomics (GA) is thrilled to announce a major accomplishment as they have finished constructing the Central Solenoid Modules, a key component of the largest and most potent pulsed superconducting magnet ever created. Standing at almost 60 feet in height, this Central Solenoid is set to drive fusion reactions at ITER, a global fusion research facility being built in southern France. This milestone highlights the innovation of California's engineering and science sector.

data.title: Snowflake (NYSE: SNOW) beats Q2 expectations, raises full-year revenue forecast. CEO claims it's the best AI data platform. Stock surges.
data.description: Snowflake, Inc., a prominent player in the cloud data warehouse industry listed as NYSE: SNOW, surpassed expectations in the second quarter in terms of top and bottom lines. They also raised their full-year product revenue forecast. The company's stock, SNOW, is experiencing significant growth. Snowflake CEO Sridhar Ramaswamy stated confidently during the latest earnings call that Snowflake is the top AI data platform available, a sentiment echoed by many existing and potential customers. Ramaswamy's assertion was supported by strong developments. Check out the real-time price fluctuations. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
